findings.description: _count_input_bytes docstring (426-427) asserts the universal 'Only successfully-read content is counted (an unreadable file contributes no bytes because none were read)' — false against HEAD: main()'s cloud-census arm (2940-2948) calls read_cloud_census which returns (None, 'unreadable/corrupt') on OSError, then unconditionally counts cloud_path.stat().st_size into input_bytes; stat() succeeds on a mode-000 file, so an unreadable snapshot contributes its full size. Every other reader honors the claim; the docstring already discloses the registry exception, making this the absolute-claim-contradicted-by-same-diff shape. Telemetry-only impact but a truthfulness defect. Fix (a): move byte accounting into read_cloud_census after successful read_text (len(text.encode('utf-8'))), matching sibling readers. Semantic dependent at 1104-1106 stays accurate once fixed.

Module docstring (29-32) says _atomic_write,_timestamp_ms,_utc_timestamp are 're-implemented here' — only _atomic_write literally is; the other two exist as functional analogues under different names (_parse_iso_ms/_ms_to_iso/_now_iso). Name the actual local equivalents.

docs/workflow-flight-recorder.md Wave-1 metrics bullet lists 'event count' among what reporting includes, but the field is unconditionally None in Wave 1 (2980). Not a falsehood (unknown-stays-null is the contract) but grants it equal status to populated figures; add '(null in Wave 1 — not aggregated across sources)'.

note: Extensive cross-file verification all byte-accurate (invocation_evidence tokens, provisional:True at wfr:1287, registry census incl. review/run nesting + matcher-probe five jobs, sample formula, stop-attempts shape, both run.sh pins).
